what_it_does: This file is an interpretive JSON analysis of AXES.md that records the parametric model of the ConceptV design system: it catalogues input axes, derived outputs, invariants, guard rules, the containment hierarchy, the zoning-as-depth unification, how each axis operates on the same tree, the typed-container capstone, and the block/graph solver unification.
observations: It frames design as the function design = f(content, axisPosition) computed over an invariant core.
It lists six orthogonal input axes: Surface, Level-of-Detail, Register/pace, Theme, Density, and Tint/gold intensity.
It separates derived outputs (type sizes, margins/gutters, reflow, tints, bullet depth, visual mode, slide count) from invariants (numbers, diagrams, brand DNA, frame signature, colour-role logic).
It states two guard rules: keep axes orthogonal, and correlation is not coupling.
It defines a containment hierarchy Deck → Slide/Frame → Section → Zone/Panel → Group/Cluster → Atom with inherited spacing rhythm.
It unifies zoning with containment depth via ~1–3% undertone shifts rather than semantic category.
It maps every axis to an operation on the same containment tree: surface=collapse, motion=temporal traversal, LOD=prune/grow, interactive=mutation, depth=elevation, theme=ground-flip.
It declares atoms, components, layout patterns, and 13 archetypes as typed composable containers at different tree depths.
It asserts block and graph are one generative substrate with two solvers: block flow/stacking and graph relational placement.
It records open questions as RESOLVED by pointing at specific source folders.
standouts: The governing equation design = f(content, axisPosition) over an invariant core.
The claim that zoning is the containment hierarchy made visible through subtle depth-keyed tinting.
The mapping of all parametric axes to operations on the same containment tree.
The declaration that block and graph are one substrate with interchangeable solvers.
The orthogonality and correlation≠coupling guard rules.
conventions: Compute-don't-author: derived outputs are computed from dials, never set directly.
Orthogonality discipline: axes compose freely and are not fused.
Single invariant core: numbers, diagrams, brand DNA, frame signature, and colour-role logic never move.
Resolve-by-source-folder: open questions are closed by pointing at specific analysed decks.
One model for deck and app: decks render the tree fixed, the app renders it mutable.
concerns: The file flags an apparent inconsistency: it cites 13 archetypes while a 25-corpus figure is referenced elsewhere.
Spacing rhythms are given as approximate ranges rather than exact tokens.
Some axes (interactive, depth/z-stack) are described conceptually without explicit data-* wiring in this file.
notes: A meta-analysis JSON that captures the parametric theory of operation for the design system, mapping every axis to operations on a single typed containment tree and unifying block/graph layout as two solvers.
questions: Is the archetype count 13 or 25, and which figure is authoritative?
Which axes are already wired as live data-* knobs versus remaining conceptual?
What exact token values correspond to the approximate spacing ranges listed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file aims to describe the parametric shape of the design system and does so across axes, outputs, invariants, containment, and solvers.
intention_signals: Inferred: to elevate the design system from a collection of hand-tuned artifacts into a generative, parametric model where a small set of orthogonal dials computes many outputs from one invariant content source, and to collapse apparently separate concepts (responsive, motion, LOD, interactivity, theming, diagrams) into operations on a single typed-container tree.
novelty: Distinctive in treating responsiveness, motion, LOD, interactivity, depth, and theming as operations on one containment tree, equating zoning with visible containment depth, and framing block-flow and graph-relational layout as the same substrate with two solvers — a strongly unified theory of a design system.
contribution: Contributes a compact parametric vocabulary (design=f(content,axis), orthogonal axes, invariant core, containment hierarchy, zoning-as-depth, per-axis tree operations, typed-container unification, block+graph solvers) that a fused system would keep to reason about how outputs are computed from content and dials.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive JSON analysis of AXES.md describing the ConceptV design system's parametric model: design as a function of content and axis positions over an invariant core, with orthogonal axes computing derived outputs, a containment hierarchy made visible through zoning, and block/graph as two solvers on one typed-container substrate.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to capture the generative theory of the design system so one content source can produce many surfaces, LODs, and themes via orthogonal dials operating on a single containment tree.
